page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate capability profile. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is weak, placing in the bottom 25% with a score of 1150.39, while multi-core performance scales to an average level of 11469.52. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance at 834.57 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 54.29 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weak at 24.73 GB/sec. The instance achieves average scores across PassMark CPU, database operations, and Redis SET operations, the latter reaching 12.97 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.cm4.4xlarge offers a stable, dedicated resource allocation suitable for legacy enterprise workloads that do not require modern AVX-512 vector instructions or local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage and weak single-core performance represent clear tradeoffs. However, the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core processing make it a viable option for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and memory read performance, though single-core CPU execution and large-block memory write speeds are weak. With average performance in PassMark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, this instance is suited for balanced, multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, moderate database tasks, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require high-speed local storage or advanced vector instruction sets.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core virtual server with balanced memory allocation, designed for standard enterprise workloads and multi-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 physical cores running at 3.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for static web serving, moderate database operations, and general-purpose workloads
